videos.title: The farmer's son became a Millionaire in Africa using 'The Shark Mentality'
videos.description:
From the dangerous mindset that no one is coming to save you to the revolutionary truth that when you realize at age 12 or 13 that your entire family is waiting for someone else to rescue them you get an awakening that changes everything, and why the brutal truth about becoming a millionaire at 25 and losing it all by 27 is that the first thing that comes to an average Ghanaian person's mind when given an opportunity in business is steal. A deep conversation about the mindset of success with Christian Amoh

00:00:00 Introduction: Meet Christian Zen Amo
00:03:17 The Awakening: Realizing No One Is Coming to Save You
00:10:42 Learning from Grandma: The Entrepreneurial Foundation
00:14:27 The Missionary Years: Finding Purpose and Building Skills
00:21:28 First Million: Breaking Into International Trade
00:28:20 The Rice Business Dream and Devastating Loss
00:33:45 The Dishonesty Problem: Why Ghanaian Entrepreneurs Struggle
00:38:12 Rising from the Ashes: The Comeback Strategy
00:43:49 The Rice Cartel: Understanding Ghana's Import Economy
00:48:28 Predatory Pricing and Market Competition
00:50:46 The Import vs Local Production Debate
00:55:12 Advice for Those Who Feel Stuck
00:57:35 The Shark Philosophy: Becoming Valuable to Winners
01:01:44 Final Wisdom: Discipline Over Motivation

Guest: Christian Zen Amoh
Company - Ohemaa Rice

Host: Derrick Abaitey

videos.title: How to Make Easy Money in Ghana | Ama Burland
videos.description:
In this raw episode of Konnected Minds, host Derrick Abaitey sits down with *Ama Burland,* the popular influencer and founder of Diya Organics who dismantles the dangerous wait until you're perfect before you post content mentality that keeps young people broke and afraid, revealing the exact moment when 10 bottles of hair oil she made for free and posted on YouTube sold out in 30 minutes after a scandal made her want to kill herself, when she struggled for two years not making money because she started with plenty orders and had to beg 20 customers when delivery services embarrassed her, when she shut down her skincare business because someone burned their face with African black soap they left on for 15 minutes instead of one minute.

00:00:00 Introduction: Meet Ama Burland
00:03:36 Growing Up: Family Background and Upbringing
00:09:57 School Days and Early Aspirations
00:17:36 The Main Character Mindset
00:21:10 Dealing with Bullying and Online Hate
00:32:00 Starting the Content Creation Journey
00:34:06 Birth of DL Organics: From Viral Moment to Business
00:51:49 Building a Business: Challenges and Growth
00:40:17 The Reality of Influencing: Money, Brands, and Sustainability
01:00:54 Advice for Young Entrepreneurs and Content Creators
